title: Sync History Entry
description: One line in ψ/memory/collaborations/<oracle>/sync.history.jsonl. Append-only (Nothing is Deleted). Stored as raw score + λ; decayedScore is computed on read via decay(raw, ts, lambda) = raw × e^(-lambda × hoursSinceTs). See issue #239 and /work-with SKILL.md § Sync Decay.

ts.description: ISO8601 timestamp when the sync was recorded (UTC, Z-suffix).

partner.description: Partner oracle id (e.g. 'mawjs', 'mawui', 'white-wormhole'). Matches contacts.json key.

raw.description: Raw sync score as confirmed by the partner at this timestamp. 0.0 = unknown/rejected, 1.0 = fully aligned. NOT decayed — decay is applied on read.

lambda.description: Decay constant λ active at the time this sync was recorded. Defaults: 0.01 intra-soul, 0.05 cross-soul, 0.10 new-relationship. Party rules may override.

node.description: Optional — which node the partner was on at sync time. Used by /fleet for cross-node edge rendering.

anchor.description: Optional — GitHub issue anchor (e.g. 'maw-js#332') when the sync was tied to a specific thread.

source.description: Optional — id of the oracle that recorded this entry (i.e. the 'from' side of the edge). Writers may omit it because the file path (ψ/memory/collaborations/<partner>/) already implies the partner; a cross-node mesh consumer (issue #235) needs both endpoints when merging multiple oracles' histories, so emit this field in any federated snapshot. Pair (source, partner) defines one directed mesh edge.
